user_prompt: Explain Bernoulli’s principle using an airplane wing with physically accurate airflow. A single commercial airplane wing in steady, level cruise flight. Scene 1: establish the wing and calm laminar airflow smoothly dividing above and below the wing. Scene 2: move closer to the airfoil cross-section, clearly showing faster, denser particle motion over the curved upper surface and slower, more spaced particle motion below, with no text or symbols. Scene 3: pull back to show the wing continuing stable flight, with lift implied through sustained motion rather than arrows or labels. Cinematic realism, continuous camera motion, consistent lighting and environment. No humans, no text, no logos, no arrows, no symbols.

style_bible.visual_style: cinematic realism with detailed textures and physically accurate airflow visualization using particle motion and subtle volumetric effects
style_bible.camera_rules: continuous smooth camera moves without jump cuts, no sudden zooms or teleports, maintain consistent perspective on the wing and airflow
style_bible.lighting_rules: soft natural daylight from above and slightly front-left, neutral color temperature, balanced contrast to highlight airflow particles and wing surface
style_bible.continuity_rules: same airplane wing and environment across all scenes, consistent lighting and particle style, smooth transitions between shots within each scene

scenes.prompt:
SCENE INTENT: Establish the airplane wing in steady flight with calm laminar airflow dividing smoothly above and below.

GLOBAL CONTINUITY (state carried across scenes):
- primary subject: single commercial airplane wing
- environment: clear blue sky with soft distant clouds
- explanatory state:
  - Scene 1: baseline condition (observation)

SHOT 1 (0–3s):
- framing: wide shot with wing centered horizontally, showing full span section
- lens: 35mm
- camera movement: slow dolly from front-left to side-right
- subject & action: by 1s, wing steady in frame; by 2s, smooth airflow particles approach and split evenly above and below wing; by 3s, particles flow laminar and parallel
- environment details: bright blue sky, faint wispy clouds far away, subtle horizon line
- lighting: soft daylight from top-left, natural shadows on wing
- cinematic look: clean film stock vibe, medium contrast, deep focus

SHOT 2 (3–7s):
- framing: medium shot focusing on mid-wing section
- lens: 50mm
- camera movement: slow pan along wing chord from leading edge to trailing edge
- subject & action: airflow particles continue smooth laminar flow, clearly dividing above and below wing surfaces
- environment details: same sky background, slight haze near horizon
- lighting: consistent soft daylight, gentle highlights on wing curvature
- cinematic look: naturalistic, balanced depth of field

SHOT 3 (7–10s):
- framing: medium-wide shot pulling back slightly to show wing and airflow
- lens: 35mm
- camera movement: slow crane upward and backward
- subject & action: airflow remains calm and steady, wing maintains level attitude
- environment details: sky with subtle cloud texture, distant horizon
- lighting: steady soft daylight
- cinematic look: realistic with smooth tonal gradation

AVOID: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit
scenes.negative_prompt: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit
scenes.camera: continuous slow dolly and pan with smooth crane pull back, maintaining wing in frame with laminar airflow particles visible
scenes.audio_cue: Here we see a commercial airplane wing in steady, level flight. Notice the smooth, calm airflow splitting evenly above and below the wing, setting the stage to understand how lift is generated.

scenes.prompt:
SCENE INTENT: Reveal the mechanism by showing faster, denser airflow particles over the curved upper wing surface and slower, more spaced particles below.

GLOBAL CONTINUITY (state carried across scenes):
- primary subject: single commercial airplane wing
- environment: clear blue sky with soft distant clouds
- explanatory state:
  - Scene 2: mechanism revealed (cause)

SHOT 1 (0–3s):
- framing: close medium shot focused on airfoil cross-section near mid-chord
- lens: 85mm
- camera movement: slow static tilt downward to reveal upper and lower surfaces
- subject & action: by 1s, airflow particles approach; by 2s, particles accelerate and cluster over upper curved surface; by 3s, particles below slow and spread apart
- environment details: soft sky background blurred for shallow depth
- lighting: soft daylight from top-left highlighting wing curvature
- cinematic look: shallow depth of field, high detail on airflow particles

SHOT 2 (3–7s):
- framing: very close shot on upper and lower wing surfaces
- lens: 85mm
- camera movement: slow pan along chord line from leading edge to trailing edge
- subject & action: airflow particles visibly faster and denser above, slower and more spaced below, showing airflow speed differences
- environment details: blurred sky background
- lighting: consistent soft daylight, subtle reflections on wing surface
- cinematic look: crisp detail on particle motion, realistic textures

SHOT 3 (7–10s):
- framing: close medium shot holding on airfoil cross-section
- lens: 85mm
- camera movement: slow static hold
- subject & action: airflow pattern stable, emphasizing speed and density differences
- environment details: soft blue sky blur
- lighting: steady soft daylight
- cinematic look: cinematic realism with clear particle motion

AVOID: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit
scenes.negative_prompt: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit
scenes.camera: slow tilt and pan with shallow depth of field focusing on airflow particle speed and density differences over wing surfaces
scenes.audio_cue: Here we zoom in on the wing's cross-section to see the cause of lift: air moves faster and more densely over the curved upper surface, while below the wing it moves slower and more spaced, creating pressure differences.

scenes.prompt:
SCENE INTENT: Show the effect of the airflow differences producing lift, with the wing continuing stable flight and airflow motion implying upward force.

GLOBAL CONTINUITY (state carried across scenes):
- primary subject: single commercial airplane wing
- environment: clear blue sky with soft distant clouds
- explanatory state:
  - Scene 3: outcome explained (effect)

SHOT 1 (0–3s):
- framing: medium-wide shot of wing from front-left angle
- lens: 35mm
- camera movement: slow dolly backward and slight upward tilt
- subject & action: wing steady in flight, airflow particles flowing faster above and slower below, maintaining smooth patterns
- environment details: blue sky with soft clouds, distant horizon
- lighting: soft daylight, natural shadows on wing
- cinematic look: realistic with balanced contrast

SHOT 2 (3–7s):
- framing: wide shot pulling back further to show more wing span
- lens: 24mm
- camera movement: slow pan right to left
- subject & action: airflow remains stable, wing level and steady, particles clearly showing continuous motion difference
- environment details: sky and clouds consistent
- lighting: steady soft daylight
- cinematic look: naturalistic, deep focus

SHOT 3 (7–10s):
- framing: medium shot holding wing steady in frame
- lens: 50mm
- camera movement: slow static hold
- subject & action: airflow patterns stable, wing maintains level flight, implying lift force sustaining the aircraft
- environment details: clear sky background
- lighting: consistent soft daylight
- cinematic look: cinematic realism

AVOID: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit
scenes.negative_prompt: text, logos, subtitles, watermarks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit
scenes.camera: smooth dolly and pan with stable framing to show wing and airflow continuing steady flight, implying lift without visual symbols
scenes.audio_cue: The faster airflow above and slower airflow below create a pressure difference that generates lift, allowing the wing to sustain stable, level flight as seen here.
